narrative_summary: Martha G. was identified in Atlantis as a renal failure transition requiring complex home monitoring. During the maintenance phase, a significant family disagreement emerged between the daughter (pro-home) and the son (pro-LTC), which I escalated to the SW for mediation. Once the SW finalized the home plan, I conducted the pulse call to verify logistics and successfully scheduled the MA for the morning after discharge to secure the first-visit incentive.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached the daughter (POA); she confirms the home is ready and she will be present for the transition. Son's concerns remain, but the SW has cleared the discharge to the daughter's care.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card successfully inserted into the patient's tablet; 'Home Care vs. SNF' program flyer delivered to both the patient and the daughter during the bedside visit.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: SW reports the patient is clinically stable, but the discharge timeline is fluid due to the son's refusal to sign off on the home plan. PN requested a post-conference summary from the SW.

label: Case_9
ma_visit_booking: Medical Assistant visit confirmed for 10/25 at 2:00 PM, within 22 hours of the patient's scheduled noon discharge.
narrative_summary: Patient was identified in Atlantis following an MS flare with a discharge plan to return home. During the maintenance phase, the primary caregiver exhibited significant anxiety regarding the transition, necessitating intensive education on the Day 1 support structure. Through a 24-hour pulse call and coordination with the HHA intake coordinator, the PN secured a visit within the incentive window and de-escalated the family's concerns.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Pulse call conducted 24 hours prior to D/C. Husband was in a state of high anxiety (Caregiver_Panic), questioning if the hospital was 'kicking them out.' PN de-escalated by confirming the HHA start-of-care time and MA visit window.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card successfully saved to the husband's contact list during the bedside visit; program flyer handed to patient and daughter.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: Patient's MS spasticity has stabilized. SW reports husband is expressing 'cold feet' about managing the patient's mobility at home; discharge target set for Friday.

atlantis_final_sync: No first visit date entered; case closed in Atlantis using the 'Neutral_LTC_Closure' status code following the SW's final determination.
demographic_audit_note: Verified 79yo male patient in Atlantis queue. Cross-check of facility face sheet revealed the primary phone number was the patient's disconnected landline; successfully updated Atlantis with son's (POA) mobile number and confirmed Medicare Advantage PPO status.

narrative_summary: Patient was admitted for amputation rehabilitation with an initial goal of returning home. The PN conducted a thorough demographic audit and provided education to the family regarding home health expectations. During the maintenance phase, the family's ability to provide wound care became a primary concern, leading the Social Worker to pivot the discharge plan from home to Long-Term Care (LTC). Per the LTC Filter policy, the PN immediately ceased all home care coordination and performed a Neutral_LTC_Closure in Atlantis.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: N/A - Pre-discharge pulse call not conducted as the case reached a 'Hard Stop' due to the LTC pivot.
v_card_and_flyer_status: Program flyer delivered to patient; V-Card successfully inserted into the son's (POA) smartphone during the Week 1 education session.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2 Check-in: SW reports PT is progressing slowly due to phantom limb pain; family is expressing anxiety regarding the upcoming discharge and the level of care required for the surgical site.

atlantis_final_sync: Entered confirmed discharge date of 11/14 and scheduled first visit for 11/15 into Atlantis.
demographic_audit_note: Audit of Atlantis queue against facility face sheet for Arthur M. (79yo M, Left BKA). Verified Humana Gold Plus (Medicare Advantage) eligibility. Identified discrepancy in primary contact phone number; updated Atlantis to reflect the daughter's (POA) current mobile number.

narrative_summary: The patient, a 79-year-old male recovering from a Left BKA, was monitored through a 14-day rehab stay. During the handoff stage, a pulse call revealed that the HHA possessed an outdated medication list, which the PN immediately flagged to the Social Worker for reconciliation. Despite this data mismatch, the PN successfully coordinated the HHA and MA schedules, ensuring the first visit occurred within 24 hours of discharge.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Conducted 24-hour pre-discharge pulse call to daughter. Discovered a critical 'Handoff_Data_Mismatch' where the HHA intake record still listed the patient's pre-surgical medication list, missing the new post-amputation anticoagulants.
v_card_and_flyer_status: Program flyer delivered to patient's bedside and V-Card successfully saved to the patient's personal smartphone contacts under 'Home Care Support'.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: Patient meeting PT goals for prosthetic gait training. SW indicates tentative discharge for Friday. PN provided education on the 24-hour first visit requirement to the patient and daughter.

demographic_audit_note: Audited Atlantis demographics against facility face sheet; corrected error in patient ZIP code and added missing secondary phone number for the daughter (POA). Verified Medicare Advantage Plan coverage for home health.

Managed significant 'Caregiver_Panic' during the Maintenance stage by providing the daughter with concrete expectations and a physical appointment card. Secured a successful transition home by scheduling a 24-hour MA follow-up, overcoming family-driven friction to trigger the first visit incentive.

narrative_summary: The patient, a 68yo female recovering from an MS flare, was tracked from intake through a complex Friday discharge. During Stage 2, I identified a regional HHA Intake Freeze that threatened the 'First Visit' window, necessitating intensive family education on the transition timeline. In Stage 3, I successfully bypassed the HHA's operational delay by scheduling our Medical Assistant for a Saturday morning visit, securing the transition incentive and ensuring the patient was not left unmonitored over the weekend.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached POA 24 hours prior to discharge. Confirmed hospital bed and O2 are in place. Alerted family that the HHA nurse visit is delayed due to the intake freeze, but our MA visit remains scheduled.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card successfully saved to patient's smartphone during bedside education session; physical program flyer delivered to daughter/POA.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: Patient completed IV steroids. SW reports gait is stabilizing. Target discharge set for Friday; however, HHA reported a temporary intake department freeze due to a regional system outage.

atlantis_final_sync: D/C Date: 08/30 (Friday); 1st Visit Date: 09/03 (Tuesday). Gap identified in Atlantis due to HHA staffing constraints over Labor Day.

Updated address and primary POA phone in Atlantis.
